name: tech-edge-ai-challenges
description: Use web_search to search for 'edge AI deployment challenges memory latency power constraints LLM'. Many results overlap with earlier edge AI searches. List the top 3 challenges for deploying LLMs on edge devices. One sentence each. Keep response under 80 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: Memory constraints are the primary barrier, as a 1.5B-parameter model with KV-cache requires ~1.2GB at 4-bit precision, and state-of-the-art LLMs demand extreme compression to fit edge device RAM. Latency and compute limitations arise because billion-parameter models require substantial arithmetic throughput that edge processors struggle to sustain in real time. Power consumption is critical since high-end GPUs draw hundreds of watts, far exceeding the 2-10W budgets of mobile and IoT devices, necessitating aggressive optimization to stay within thermal and battery limits.
key_facts: A 1.5B-parameter model with 2K token KV-cache requires approximately 1.2GB of memory at 4-bit weights
Edge devices are typically limited to 2-10W power consumption versus hundreds of watts for datacenter GPUs
Billion-parameter models pose a significant barrier due to high memory footprint and computation cost on resource-constrained devices
Bandwidth limitations further compound the challenge as LLMs require large data throughput that edge devices cannot easily provide
